story.title: Dr. Yuki Tanaka and the Volcano's Secret Message
story.text:
Hi, I’m Dr. Yuki Tanaka. I study how volcanoes change our world. It’s like being a detective for the planet.

A volcano is part of the geosphere, or the solid Earth. When it erupts, it connects the geosphere to the atmosphere, which is the air all around us. It sends a giant cloud of gas and ash into the sky. My job is to figure out what that cloud does to our climate.

The cloud has two important ingredients. First, it has a greenhouse gas called carbon dioxide. This gas can trap heat and make the world a little warmer over a very long time.

But there's a second, faster-acting ingredient: a gas called sulfur dioxide. This gas makes tiny particles that act like a sun-blocking shield. These particles float high in the atmosphere. They reflect sunlight back into space. This can make the whole planet cooler for one or two years!

So, a volcano on one continent can change the weather everywhere. My computer models help me predict these big changes. It shows how all of Earth’s systems are connected.
think_abouts: Why is it important to study both the warming and cooling effects of a volcano?
What are the four 'spheres' of Earth mentioned in the background information?
story.go_deeper: If a huge volcano erupted and cooled the planet for two years, how might that affect the plants and animals near you?

margin_defs.definition: The solid parts of Earth, like rocks and mountains.

check_prompts: What are two ingredients in a volcanic cloud that can change the climate?

vocabulary.definition: All the rock, mountains, and land that make up the solid part of Earth.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A volcano is a part of the geosphere that can blast rock high into the air.

vocabulary.definition: The layer of air and gases that surrounds our planet.
vocabulary.use_sentence: When we breathe, we are taking in gases from the atmosphere.

vocabulary.definition: When a change in a system causes another change that pushes the system back toward where it started.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A thermostat in a house is like a negative feedback loop because it turns off the heat when it gets too warm.

vocabulary.definition: A gas in the air, like carbon dioxide, that traps heat and helps keep Earth warm.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Volcanoes release the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Tanaka uses computer models to predict climate changes. What are some other tools a scientist who studies volcanoes might use?
discussion_cards.frame: A scientist studying volcanoes might also use tools like...

discussion_cards.prompt: The story says volcano particles can cool the Earth. How is this an example of the geosphere changing the atmosphere and the climate?
discussion_cards.frame: The geosphere changes the atmosphere when...

discussion_cards.prompt: The story mentions two opposite effects: warming from CO2 and cooling from other particles. Why do you think it is important for scientists to study both effects?
discussion_cards.frame: It is important to study both effects because...

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
